zh.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard De Elye於1245年之前與Richard De Elye在英國結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard De Elye于1245年之前与Richard De Elye在英国结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Elye
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Ells   or   Ellis  .
North German: topographic name for someone who lived by an alder or alders, Middle Low German else, or a habitational name from any of various places formed with this word.

variants.name: Els
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Ewald, Wilhelm.

variants.name: Ells
meanings: English (London and Kent): variant of Ellis , or a variant of Ell , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard De Elye married Richard De Elye before 1245, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard De Elye a épousé Richard De Elye avant 1245, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard De Elye hat vor 1245 in England Richard De Elye geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard De Elye lei sposato Richard De Elye prima del 1245, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia.

ja.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard De Elyeは1245年以前にイギリスでRichard De Elyeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard De Elye(은)는 1245년 이전 영국에서 Richard De Elye(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard De Elye casou-se com Richard De Elye antes 1245, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard De Elye вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard De Elye перед 1245 г. в местности Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard De Elye se casó con Richard De Elye antes de 1245, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija.
